metric_name: Career transition from PHI to ADL
metric_value: Kastner joined ADL Systems in Cambridge after Wang Laboratories announced plans to move PHI to Tewksbury, triggering PHI diaspora.
PHI alumni arrived as 'small, seasoned rescue squad, hired precisely because we knew how systems actually worked rather than how they were supposed to work.'
At PHI, staff lived inside the machine—core dumps, disk-arm rotations, microsecond counts. Machine was the universe with rules.
Most systems don’t fail in the machine; they fail in the boardroom, the warehouse, or the customs shed at the airport.
Migrate Chase Manhattan Bank securities processing (~$20B in assets) from aging Univac hardware to IBM without losing a single share or penny.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_name: Chase Manhattan securities assets under management
metric_value: Approximately $20 billion in assets in securities processing operation.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Equipment described as 'archeologically obsolete'; spare parts legendarily dumped at sea; impossible to source replacement hardware at any price.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Two Cosmos experts delivered 25 million lines of bug-free COBOL code in one year.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Univac 96-column cards vs. IBM 80-column: every Chase field designed for 96 columns had to be exploded into multiple 80-column IBM cards, rebuilding every field boundary.
notes: 1970s equivalent of cross-vendor database format migration.

metric_value: Rockefeller’s holdings were so large that the standard dividend field could not contain the numbers after column-count reduction; team spent hours hunting for one more column.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Chase assembled a small army of temp workers to reprocess days of transactions by hand to verify both Univac and IBM systems produced identical answers.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Automation is usually born not from a love of elegance but from sheer exhaustion with manual labor.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: Architecture built for Chase ran for decades, surviving corporate mergers and hardware generations; DNA traces persist in modern financial plumbing.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_value: By 1975 Kastner had become a ‘vetted asset’; clients named in proposals began requesting him personally.

metric_name: Federal University Hospital Rio project mandate
metric_value: Turnkey hospital system to serve an urban poverty area in Rio de Janeiro; client insisted Kastner be on the project after reading his biography in the proposal.

metric_name: Brazil foreign exchange crisis impact on IT design
metric_value: Brazil’s currency controls made importing American computer hardware nearly impossible; online terminal design adapted to paper forms and bicycle couriers.
Instead of terminals: paper forms; instead of data packets: young men on bicycles pedaling batch-input forms several km from hospital to computer center.
notes: Kastner’s coined concept for constraints-driven low-tech solution.

metric_value: Kastner debugged Burroughs code in 104-degree heat; air conditioners stripped from building; notes stained with sweat, ink ran.
Brazilian customs inspector unspooled magnetic tape reel on floor searching for forbidden images; tape at 800 bpi looks nothing like a photograph.

metric_name: Technology as vital organ, not whole creature
metric_value: Rio trip changed how Kastner thought: software is a vital organ; the system is a living organism embedded in a hostile environment—power outages, currency crises, bad roads, bad policy.
The technology was never the limiting factor. The operating environment was.
ADL sent Kastner into paper mills, electric utilities, municipal police departments with expectation he’d understand operations quickly enough to design systems for proposals.

metric_name: Continental Forest Industries paper mill engagement
metric_value: Mill produced 2,000-lb kraft paper rolls in continuous industrial ballet; inventory-control system had to decide quickly whether to ship roll or divert to cardboard line.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Lucille Ball Problem

metric_value: If the routing decision took too long, the floor ran out of space; 2,000-lb roll is a one-ton projectile. Software’s job was to be an unseen choreographer.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Lucille Ball Problem

metric_value: Like the Lucille Ball chocolate-factory sketch—except the chocolates weighed a ton and could kill someone; hesitation in software was fatal.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Lucille Ball Problem

metric_name: Wisconsin Electric remote meter reading challenge
metric_value: Rural service territory connected by party telephone lines and tight capital budgets; dedicated data lines financially impossible.
Proposed using power distribution lines as communication medium; low-frequency signals superimposed on 60-Hz current; 5 baud—no new wiring required.
5 bits per second is roughly one billion times slower than a modern USB connection; bandwidth was not the point—meters needed to report infrequently and honestly.
Best solution fits physical and economic constraints of actual system, not engineer’s ambitions. Embracing appropriate technology is not settling—it is respecting reality.

metric_name: Hampton VA police information system transfer assessment
metric_value: Sent to Hampton, VA to verify whether police information system could be transferred to St. Petersburg, FL; given police radio with officer-needs-assistance code.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Police Radio Problem

metric_name: Safety warning from Vice Chief of Police
metric_value: ‘Don’t leave your room at night,’ said the Vice Chief of Police; Kastner spent more mental energy on door lock integrity than database schema.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Police Radio Problem

metric_value: Evaluating Hampton VA police system for transfer to St. Petersburg, FL; assessment included intellectual property verification.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Police Radio Problem

metric_name: Consequences of specification error in public safety
metric_value: Police system specification errors would be measured not only in dollars but in officers’ lives if warrant query returned incorrect results.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Police Radio Problem

metric_value: As decade wore on, ADL stopped sending Kastner to design new systems and started sending him as consultant to explain why existing systems were on fire.

metric_name: Personal journaling practice on troubled engagements
metric_value: Kept detailed personal journals on every troubled engagement as a black-box recorder; more than once called as witness.

metric_name: Programmer skills translate to forensic consulting
metric_value: Paranoia, attention to edge cases, obsession with what logs actually say rather than what they should say translate surprisingly well into forensic consulting work.
IT manager fired; new system turned on Jan 1 for accounting convenience; company weeks from being unable to close books; hardware was Honeywell.

metric_name: IBM tool incompatibility at Honeywell site
metric_value: Every IBM-era tool and habit Kastner had developed over a decade was suddenly useless on Honeywell hardware.

metric_name: Root cause of technology project failure
metric_value: Real danger in tech projects is rarely technical; it is political, managerial, financial misalignment wrapped around mis-scoped, mis-priced, or mis-governed projects.
ADLS management decided to migrate IBM 4300 mainframe code to Data General Eclipse minicomputer; violated architectural physics of both platforms.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_value: System thrashing at every level: CPU, application layer, disks, and boardroom; migration was in production managing real business transactions.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_value: Every application contains thousands of implicit assumptions about hardware (memory addressing, I/O channels, interrupt timing, FP precision) never documented because developers never imagined migration.
source_page: Sidebar: Why Platform Migration Is Rarely Just a Porting Exercise

metric_name: Platform migration approved by uninformed management
metric_value: United Shoe migration was not a software project gone wrong; it was an architectural mismatch approved by people who did not understand the physics.
source_page: Sidebar: Why Platform Migration Is Rarely Just a Porting Exercise

metric_value: Working with Frank Hawthorne (ADL Systems GE-trained CFO), Kastner conducted midyear audit of firm’s OEM contracts.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_name: ADL Systems OEM earnings inflation discovery
metric_value: Management had been booking volume discounts as instant profit before the sales volume generating those discounts had been achieved—a classic earnings inflation, pending liability.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_name: Bad accounting kills faster than bad code
metric_value: Reporting the OEM accounting problem was a defining professional moment; crystallized conviction: bad accounting can kill a company faster than bad code.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_value: Started trading Wang Laboratories stock options in his early twenties; watched technology market rhythms with increasing attention.
ADL specialized in fixed-price turnkey contracts when system complexity was exploding beyond reliable scoping; capped upside (fixed fee) while leaving downside open.

metric_name: Lawsuit risk from fixed-price custom development
metric_value: ADL was taking equity risk for consulting fees on fixed-price custom development; Kastner could see lawsuits coming.
DEC, Data General, Prime, and a dozen others were selling machines real businesses could operate without a priesthood of systems programmers.

metric_name: Minicomputer revolution: computing escaping the glass house
metric_value: Minicomputer revolution was gathering force; computing escaping the glass house; economic center of gravity shifting from system builders to designers and sellers.

metric_name: Kastner leaves ADL for Prime Computer
metric_value: Left Arthur D. Little and walked into the marketing offices of Prime Computer; left coding pad behind.

metric_name: Inversion of computing economics by end of 1970s
metric_value: In the world Kastner was trained for, computer time was scarce and human labor cheap. By end of 1970s that equation was inverting: human judgment based on experience was becoming the scarce resource.
Eight years at Arthur D. Little (1972–1979); chapter also references 'seven years' in one passage, indicating departure ca. 1979.
notes: Chapter title says eight years; body text says seven years at one point. Both consistent with 1972-1979 span.

metric_value: Over ADL years, Kastner saw systems fail technically, organizationally, financially, politically; wrote code in Boston winters and Rio heat waves.
Kastner interviewed paper-mill foremen, police dispatchers, bank auditors, and utility engineers during ADL years.

metric_name: Murray Sherry as trusted colleague in PHI-ADL transition
metric_value: Kastner followed Murray Sherry and others from PHI to ADL Systems; described as people he trusted.
Bug-free COBOL code was the currency of the Chase migration; 25 million lines delivered in one year by two Cosmos experts.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money

metric_name: Burroughs code debugging in Rio heat
metric_value: Kastner debugged Burroughs code in 104-degree heat in project office with stripped air conditioners; notes stained with sweat.
The ability to understand the messy interface between technology and organizational reality was the asset Kastner intended to compound at Prime Computer.

metric_name: ADL Systems OEM contract risk structure
metric_value: Midyear audit revealed earnings inflation from pre-booking volume discounts as instant profit; a pending liability that would eventually be reversed.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_name: Kitchen knife analogy: craft vs. leverage
metric_value: Felt like a man who’d spent a decade mastering the kitchen knife—skilled, proud of craft—only to realize real leverage had migrated to whoever designed the menu and owned the franchise.

metric_name: Departure from coding as recognition not repudiation
metric_value: Leaving the coding pad was not a repudiation of the work; it was recognition that the physics of the industry had changed.

metric_name: Univac spare parts supply chain collapse
metric_value: Legend: spare parts for Chase Manhattan’s Univac had literally been dumped at sea; replacement hardware unavailable at any price.
source_page: Chase Manhattan: The Cardboard Physics of Money
notes: Kastner notes this may be legend, not verified fact.

metric_name: Fixed-price consulting vs. custom development risk
metric_value: Selling fixed-price custom development is not selling applications; it is fix-pricing custom development with open downside; equity risk for consulting fees.
Two-thousand-pound (one-ton) rolls of kraft paper; continuous motion at speeds turning each roll into a projectile.
source_page: Invisible Factories – The Lucille Ball Problem

metric_value: Rural meter reading at 5 baud on power lines: matching solution to infrastructure at hand is not settling for second best—it is respecting reality.

metric_name: Rio project online terminal design vs. constraint reality
metric_value: Original design: online terminals connecting hospital to central data center. Adapted to paper forms and bicycle couriers due to Brazil currency controls on hardware imports.
‘Turn on the new system on January 1 because that’s easy for accounting.’ IT manager already fired; company weeks from being unable to close books.

metric_name: Systematic failure taxonomy: boardroom, warehouse, customs shed
metric_value: Over ADL years Kastner developed taxonomy of system failure loci: boardroom (political/financial), warehouse (operational), customs shed (regulatory/environmental).

metric_name: Transition from coder to market shaper
metric_value: Could keep perfecting technique or step into a role to shape the market itself; chose latter by joining Prime Computer marketing.
GE-trained CFO of ADL Systems; collaborated with Kastner on midyear OEM contract audit.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_name: IBM 4300 as United Shoe Machinery source platform
metric_value: Code written for IBM 4300 mainframe; ADLS management decided to migrate to Data General Eclipse without understanding architectural mismatch.
source_page: The Forensic Turn – United Shoe Machinery

metric_value: DEC named alongside Data General and Prime as leaders of minicomputer revolution letting businesses operate without systems programmers priesthood.

metric_name: Prime Computer role in minicomputer revolution
metric_value: Named as one of the dozen minicomputer vendors selling machines real businesses could operate without a priesthood of systems programmers.
